entries.source_text: Selectable secondary provider via a registry (openai gpt-5.6-sol / zai glm-5.2 first entries; future providers = one entry)
scenarios: choosing Z.ai routes the secondary alias to GLM 5.2
acceptance_criteria: hermetic: install with --secondary zai --zai-key <fake> generates the config route openai/glm-5.2 + api_base https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4 + os.environ/ZAI_API_KEY; raw key only in gateway.env (pinned key-free elsewhere)
hermetic: registry entries drive key slots + labels; adding a fake provider entry in-test yields a working route without code changes

live (openai path, AMENDED 2026-07-17 per the user's subscription directive — transient sandboxed run, machine left subscription-state; see .architect-team/clarifications/secondary-provider-registry-20260717T0705Z.md): sandboxed install + gateway start ends status --live CONFIRMED — serving 32 models incl. ct6-secondary + claude-fable-5

entries.source_text: A which-secondary-API question on three surfaces, remembered in gateway.json, re-asked only via --re-ask-provider; pre-upgrade installs grandfathered as openai
scenarios: the which-provider question fires only when no choice is recorded
pre-upgrade installs are grandfathered as openai
acceptance_criteria: hermetic: resolution ladder pins — flag > env > recorded > grandfather > interactive > default-openai-recorded; recorded/inferred choice NEVER re-prompts absent --re-ask-provider
hermetic: a state with openai_model and no secondary_provider infers openai with no prompt call (prompt seam counter = 0)
wrapper: commands/architect-team-setup.md carries the AskUserQuestion section with the firing rule + capture-and-apply re-run (structure pinned by tests/test_commands.py conventions)

entries.source_text: Provider-keyed key capture (ZAI_API_KEY) rides the v3.38.0 ask-then-apply machinery; missing chosen-provider key yields provisioned-but-NOT-enabled
scenarios: provider-keyed key capture rides the existing machinery
acceptance_criteria: hermetic: zai slot prompts via the injected getpass seam (interactive TTY only, blank-to-skip, decline recorded/auto-reset/--re-ask-keys), exactly mirroring the openai slot's existing pins
hermetic: zai chosen + no ZAI_API_KEY => enabled False + remediation naming the zai slot

entries.source_text: Provider-neutral alias ct6-secondary replaces codex-5.6-sol globally (one alias for both providers) with legacy recognition + migration via setup AND the SessionStart self-heal; ship state stays uniform fable
scenarios: the split writes the neutral alias
policy state recognizes both alias generations
the deprecated split invocation still works
acceptance_criteria: hermetic: apply_split writes ct6-secondary (18 fable / 21 ct6-secondary on a real agents copy); policy_state classifies new AND legacy split states; --split codex synonym works with a deprecation note; uniform lever refuses both aliases
hermetic: sessionstart heal rewrites a legacy-alias installed-copy fixture to ct6-secondary; uninstall restores uniform fable from either alias

live (AMENDED 2026-07-17, transient sandboxed): a plain re-install over a scratch agents dir seeded with codex-5.6-sol migrated 2 dev agents to model: ct6-secondary (fable file untouched, honest alias-migration row); the REAL installed plugin copy deliberately stays uniform fable per the user's subscription directive
ship-state pin: all 39 committed agents remain model: fable (tests/test_agents.py)

entries.source_text: Subscription mode honors the provider choice; confirm/status/uninstall symmetric; postures verbatim; suite green both encodings; v3.40.0 + docs
acceptance_criteria: hermetic: subscription-mode install with zai chosen serves glm-5.2 to direct callers (config shape), split stays OFF, ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL never written
full pytest suite green under BOTH Windows cp1252 AND PYTHONUTF8=1
docs current per house convention at v3.40.0 (doc-currency audit pass); never-gates/never-sudo/secrets/consent postures pinned unchanged

playwright_authorization: N/A — no frontend surface (plugin repo, no UI); recorded per Phase 1's N/A convention. The 'question that pops up' is an AskUserQuestion in a Claude session + a TTY prompt — covered by wrapper-structure pins and hermetic prompt-seam tests, not browser flows.
integration_authorization: The zai path's LIVE end-to-end confirm requires a Z.ai API key not present on this machine — hermetic coverage + the openai path live-verified stand in; recorded honestly per design.md Dev Environment (the same provisioned-but-not-enabled boundary the gateway already documents).
notes: Feature run; layer infra throughout (setup/infra scripts + docs + tests; no product frontend/backend).
